description_long: This cabin features a combination of rustic and modern architectural elements, with a prominent stone chimney on one side and a mix of red wooden planks and shingle siding. The roof is split into two distinct sections: a traditional gable roof over the main structure and a curved, barrel-vaulted roof over an attached side section. The main entrance appears to be under the curved roof, which is supported by wooden beams and opens onto a gravel base. The cabin's exterior is finished with a blend of natural stone, wood, and shingle textures, giving it a cozy yet sturdy appearance. There are no visible windows, and the doors are simple, rectangular, and blend into the siding. The structure sits on a flat, stone-textured platform, suggesting it is designed for placement in an urban or suburban environment.
description: A rustic cabin with a stone chimney, red wood siding, and a mix of gable and barrel-vaulted roofs, set on a gravel base. The exterior combines wood, stone, and shingles for a cozy, sturdy look.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stone chimney, red wood siding, and the transition between the gable and barrel-vaulted roofs.
description_view_1: From this angle, the curved barrel-vaulted roof and the shingle siding are most prominent, with the end of the cabin visible.
description_view_2: The rear view shows the white panel siding and the open end of the barrel-vaulted roof, with the gable roof above.
description_view_3: This side view emphasizes the wooden gable roof, red wood siding, and the stone chimney at the edge.
attribute_roof_type: dual roof: gable roof over main section and barrel-vaulted (curved) roof over side section, both with visible wooden and shingle materials
attribute_window_arrangement: No visible windows on any side; exterior surfaces are solid wood, stone, or shingle panels
attribute_door_placement: Single exterior door located under the barrel-vaulted roof section, adjacent to the main structure
